lines.english: The ultimate action is to contemplate the Word of the Shabad. ||3||
The mind becomes pure, when the True Lord dwells within.
When one dwells in Truth, all actions become true.
The Giver, the Great Giver, lives forever.
How rare is that Gurmukh who recognizes the Naam, the Name of the Lord.
Through the Guru, true service is performed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਪਾਏ ਜਨੁ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur te giaan paae jan koe |
lines.english: Those who obtain spiritual wisdom from the Guru are very rare.
Gauree Gwaarayree, Third Mehl:
Nanak enshrines love for the Name of the Lord. ||4||1||21||
Through the Guru, the Gate of Liberation is found. ||1||
Through perfect good destiny, we come to meet the Guru.
Those who obtain this understanding from the Guru become acceptable.
Through the Guru, we intuitively contemplate the True One.
Meeting the Guru, the fire of desire is quenched.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਏ ॥
lines.translit: gur te saant vasai man aae |
lines.english: Through the Guru, peace and tranquility come to dwell within the mind.
The true ones are intuitively absorbed in the True Lord. ||1||Pause||
Without the Guru, everyone wanders in doubt.
Through the Guru, we are absorbed in the Word of the Shabad. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਪਵਿਤ ਪਾਵਨ ਸੁਚਿ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur te pavit paavan such hoe |
lines.english: Through the Guru, we become pure, holy and true.
Those who meditate on the Naam become Gurmukh.
Without the Name, they suffer in terrible pain.
True honor is obtained through the Darshan, the Blessed Vision of the True Lord. ||3||
When He grants His Grace, union with the Shabad is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਹੀਐ ਦਾਤਾ ਇਕੁ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: kis no kaheeai daataa ik soee |
lines.english: Why speak of any other? He alone is the Giver.
Meeting with my Beloved, I sing the Glorious Praises of the True Lord.
O Nanak, becoming true, I am absorbed in the True One. ||4||2||22||
Gauree Gwaarayree, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁ ਥਾਉ ਸਚੁ ਮਨੁ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: su thaau sach man niramal hoe |
lines.english: True is that place, where the mind becomes pure.
True is the one who abides in Truth.
The True Bani of the Word is known throughout the four ages.
The True One Himself is everything. ||1||
Through the karma of good actions, one joins the Sat Sangat, the True Congregation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ਬੈਸਿ ਸੁ ਥਾਏ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har gun gaavai bais su thaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Sing the Glories of the Lord, sitting in that place. ||1||Pause||
Burn this tongue, which loves duality,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਨ ਚਾਖੈ ਫੀਕਾ ਆਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har ras na chaakhai feekaa aalaae |
lines.english: which does not taste the sublime essence of the Lord, and which utters insipid words.
Without the Name, the miserable ones depart crying out in pain. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਬੂਝੇ ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਫੀਕਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin boojhe tan man feekaa hoe |
lines.english: Without understanding, the body and mind become tasteless and insipid.
By Guru's Grace, is absorbed in the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਚਾਖਿਆ ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: rasanaa har ras chaakhiaa sehaj subhaae |
lines.english: One whose tongue naturally and intuitively tastes the Lord's sublime essence,
Nothing is collected if the vessel is upside-down.
and drinks in the Ambrosial Nectar, from the immaculate stream within. ||3||
The Naam, the Name of the Lord, is collected in the vessel of the mind.
Imbued with Truth, one contemplates the Word of the Guru's Shabad,
Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, the Naam abides within the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੁ ਭਾਂਡਾ ਜਿਸੁ ਸਬਦ ਪਿਆਸ ॥੪॥੩॥੨੩॥
lines.translit: naanak sach bhaanddaa jis sabad piaas |4|3|23|
lines.english: O Nanak, True is that vessel of the mind, which thirsts for the Shabad. ||4||3||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਗਾਵਤ ਰਹੇ ਮਨਿ ਸਾਦੁ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: eik gaavat rahe man saad na paae |
lines.english: Some sing on and on, but their minds do not find happiness.
Gauree Gwaarayree, Third Mehl:
In egotism, they sing, but it is wasted uselessly.
Those who love the Naam, sing the song.
They contemplate the True Bani of the Word, and the Shabad. ||1||
They sing on and on, if it pleases the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੁਹਾਵੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: man tan raataa naam suhaavai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Their minds and bodies are embellished and adorned, attuned to the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||1||Pause||
Some sing, and some perform devotional worship.
True devotional worship consists of love for the Word of the Guru's Shabad.
Without heart-felt love, the Naam is not obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨਾ ਪਿਰੁ ਰਾਖਿਆ ਸਦਾ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: apanaa pir raakhiaa sadaa ur dhaar |2|
lines.english: The devotee keeps his Beloved clasped tightly to his heart. ||2||
